title: activity — authorization policies
description: Two policy classes. `ActivityPolicy` gates reads on activity rows (writes never happen over HTTP — the vendor trait is the only write path). `ActivityRetentionPolicyPolicy` gates CRUD on retention overrides. Guard-aware — each ability checks the caller's guard (sanctum / platform_admin) and gates accordingly.

abilities.name: viewAny
abilities.description: List activities in the caller's tenant. RBAC-narrowed by `activity.enforce_scope` middleware — the caller sees activities on entities they hold viewing permissions for + their own causer stream.

abilities.name: view
requires: activity.viewAny AND (activity.tenant_id === auth_user.tenant_id) AND (activity.causer_id === auth_user.id OR caller has viewing permission on activity.subject OR caller.has('activity.audit_any'))
abilities.description: Show a specific activity. Tenant-scoped + subject-scoped OR admin-audit-scoped.

abilities.name: viewOwn
abilities.description: The caller's own activity as causer. Available to every authenticated tenant user — the /activities/mine endpoint is always accessible.

abilities.name: viewByEntity
requires: caller can view the entity itself (the entity's own policy is invoked to determine subject visibility)
abilities.description: Enumerate activity on a specific entity. Enforced via delegation to the entity's own policy — 'a booking's activity is visible to anyone who can view the booking'.

abilities.name: viewByUser
abilities.description: Enumerate activities where a specific user is causer. Callers see their own; admins with `activity.audit_any` see any user in the tenant.

abilities.name: subscribeFeed
abilities.description: Authorize WebSocket subscription to activity broadcast channels. Enforced by the reverb channel auth callback — the sanctum request must present a valid PAT AND hold `activity.viewAny`.

abilities.name: export
requires: activity.export AND tenant entitled to `activity_export` (medium plan+)
abilities.description: Trigger an asynchronous CSV export. Entitlement check runs alongside permission check.

abilities.name: viewAnyPlatform
abilities.description: Cross-tenant list for Academorix ops. Every list read captures the (platform_user_id, tenant_ids_touched, filters) tuple in the audit stream.

abilities.name: viewAny
abilities.description: List retention policies. Admin-level compliance surface.

abilities.name: create
requires: activity.retention.manage AND (retention_days <= 90 OR tenant entitled to `activity_retention_extended`)
abilities.description: Create a retention override. Entitlement gate — tenants without the entitlement can only create policies at the default (90d) or shorter; requests with retention_days > 90 return 402 GRANT_RETENTION_NOT_ENTITLED.

abilities.name: update
requires: activity.retention.manage AND policy.tenant_id === auth_user.tenant_id AND (updated_retention_days <= 90 OR tenant entitled)
abilities.description: Patch a policy. Cannot change tenant_id or log_name post-create; only retention_days + override_reason + expires_at are patchable.

abilities.name: delete
abilities.description: Soft-delete a policy. Reverts the (tenant, log_name) pair to the config default; a purge sweep may fire immediately if the default is more restrictive.

notes: The `viewByEntity` ability DELEGATES to the entity's own policy — that's the RBAC-scope enforcement. If a caller can't view a specific booking, they can't view its activity stream. This is enforced at the query layer in `activity.enforce_scope` middleware, not at the policy layer alone.
Every read is captured. Even a Gate::denies on `activity.view` is captured on the audit stream as a `preflight_failed` event — a denied peek at someone else's activity is a security signal.

There is no `create` / `update` / `delete` ability on `ActivityPolicy` — writes happen exclusively via the vendor `LogsActivity` trait. Attempts to POST /api/v1/activities return 405 Method Not Allowed.
